claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit is assessed on a per-child basis, so I'll need to know about each child you're inquiring about.

**First question: Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for? If yes, how old is the child (or how old are the children)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Since Alex is 13, he's under 16 years old. Now I need to ask a few more questions:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. He's only 13, so he's still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular 13-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex in any kind of apprenticeship program?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. He's only 13, so he's just doing his regular schooling.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's clear, thank you. Now I need to ask some questions about your own circumstances.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, I need to check if you meet any special circumstances that would still make you eligible.

**Are you a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces) living abroad, or are you living in certain qualifying countries while working for the UK government?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or working for the UK government. I'm just living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Let me ask one more question about your residency status:

**Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me review your situation carefully.

Based on what you've told me:
- You have a son, Alex, who is 13 years old
- Alex is in school, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship
- You are living abroad
- You are not a Crown servant or working for the UK government abroad
- You do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

**Unfortunately, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit in this situation.**

The reason is that one of the key eligibility requirements for Child Benefit is that the claimant must meet UK residency requirements. This typically means you must:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Meet certain special circumstances (such as being a Crown servant abroad, or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with certain conditions met)

Since you are living abroad and do not meet any of the special circumstance criteria, you do not satisfy the residency requirements for claiming Child Benefit, even though Alex would otherwise qualify as an eligible child.

If your circumstances change and you move back to the UK or take up a qualifying role abroad (such as Crown service), you could become eligible at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?
